note: Clinical content (CLAUDE.md §9, dual-assessment architecture 2026-07-11) — PLACEHOLDER COPY pending advisor sign-off, see docs/clinical-review/2026-07-11-dual-assessment-architecture.md. Templated into ComparisonResult.narrative by @earlysteps/comparison-engine — never LLM-generated. statuses/reasons cover the CLAUDE.md §13 vocabulary verbatim (agreement/partial_agreement/disagreement; unsupported_text_evidence/contradictory_responses/insufficient_evidence/missing_observations/low_confidence/conflicting_developmental_history). red_flag_safety_note is prepended whenever a red flag is present, regardless of computed status, so this section can never read as softening one (CLAUDE.md §2 rule 8).

statuses.agreement: The official screening result and this independent AI read point in the same general direction.
statuses.partial_agreement: The official screening result and this independent AI read are broadly in the same range, but not a close match.
statuses.disagreement: The official screening result and this independent AI read point in noticeably different directions.

reasons.unsupported_text_evidence: The AI read draws on typed notes and free-text answers that the official screening result doesn't use — that can shift the two apart.
reasons.contradictory_responses: Some of the typed answers this session don't fully line up with each other.
reasons.insufficient_evidence: There isn't yet enough answered evidence for the official screening result to compare closely against.
reasons.missing_observations: A few areas still have too few answers for a full comparison.
reasons.low_confidence: At least one side of this comparison is working from limited or uncertain evidence right now.
reasons.conflicting_developmental_history: Some details about development over time point in different directions.

red_flag_safety_note: A specific serious-sign answer was given directly by the caregiver. That finding stands no matter what this comparison shows.
